scenario: A growing organisation has 200 projects in Web Modeler. A team lead wants to search across all projects for processes referencing a specific Connector or variable name.
question: Does Web Modeler support cross-project search?

options.text: Verify per version: Web Modeler typically supports search within a project, but cross-project / org-wide search capabilities vary. Workarounds: - Web Modeler API: list all projects programmatically; for each, list resources; download contents; grep / search locally. - Git-synced content: if projects sync to Git, search the Git repo directly with standard tools (grep, IDE search). - Search UI: some Web Modeler versions add cross-project search features. "), Git-based search is often the most practical.

Claiming that Web Modeler has fully built-in cross-project search is only partially correct and version-dependent. 8, native cross-project search across all 200 projects in an organization may not be available as a single UI feature. Project-level search allowing users to find resources within a single project is the typical baseline. Cross-project search capabilities may exist as a partial feature, a preview, or require workarounds involving the Web Modeler API or Git-synced content. Teams requiring comprehensive cross-project resource discovery should not assume this feature is fully available without verifying their specific Camunda 8 version's capabilities.

Web Modeler typically provides at minimum project-level search allowing users to find process definitions, DMN files, form schemas, and other resources within a given project by name or content keywords. The absence of native cross-project search does not mean no search exists; it means the search scope is limited to a project boundary. For most daily development workflows, project-level search is sufficient, and cross-project discovery is an administrative or governance concern addressed through workarounds.

Claiming that Web Modeler search is limited exclusively to searching by file name is only partially accurate. While searching by resource name is a common and baseline search capability in Web Modeler, modern versions typically offer more than just filename matching. Content-based search, searching by BPMN element IDs, or searching within process variable names may be available depending on the version. Additionally, the Web Modeler API enables programmatic exploration of project contents beyond simple filename queries. For cross-project scenarios, Git Sync enables full-text search across all file content using standard Git and IDE search tools.

a.text: Correct. Web Modeler search capabilities evolve across versions: Project-level search: typical baseline -- search within a project for resource names / content. Cross-project search: may or may not be a native feature depending on version. Workarounds: Workaround 1: Web Modeler API `python # Pseudo-code for project in list_projects(): for resource in list_resources(project): content = download_resource(resource) if "ConnectorX" in content: print(f"Found in {project.name}/{resource.name}") Workaround 2: Git-synced content If projects use Git Sync, content is in a Git repo. Standard tools: git grep "ConnectorX"`, IDE's project-wide search. Often the most practical for discovery / governance. Workaround 3: Custom dashboards Build internal tools that aggregate Web Modeler content; index for search. Use cases: Deprecation: "which BPMNs use Connector X v1?" before deprecating. Refactoring: find all uses of a Form / DMN before changing. Audit: find process references for compliance. Best practice: combine Web Modeler with Git Sync for project-wide content + Git-based search. Camunda-native tooling may catch up over versions.
